The exact classification of Gronophyllum flabellatum includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its order as Malpighiales. intro: Gronophyllum flabellatum, also known as East Indian rosewood or East Indian rosewood oil, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Gronophyllum flabellatum include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as skin infections and pain relief. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
